question: Does the output include exactly four distinct schematic diagrams, one corresponding to each of the required roof types: Wudian, Xieshan, Xuanshan, and Yingshan?
Does the text accurately define the Wudian roof (hip roof) as having five ridges (one main ridge and four hip ridges) and four sloping surfaces, without any vertical gable walls?
Does the text accurately define the Xieshan roof (hip-and-gable roof) as a composite structure featuring a hip roof section on top and gable walls below, characterized by nine ridges?

Does the text correctly distinguish Xuanshan (overhanging gable) from Yingshan (flush gable) by stating that Xuanshan eaves extend beyond the gable wall, whereas Yingshan eaves end flush with the wall?
Does the generated text correctly establish the hierarchical ranking of the roof forms in the order of Wudian (highest) > Xieshan > Xuanshan > Yingshan (lowest)?

Does the text explicitly explain the logic that Wudian and Xieshan forms were reserved for higher-status imperial or religious buildings, while Xuanshan and Yingshan were typically restricted to civilian residences?
Does the output identify the feudal "ritual system" (Li/social hierarchy) as the underlying logic dictating these architectural restrictions?
Does the text use appropriate professional terminology (e.g., Chinese terms like 'Zhengji', 'Chuiji', 'Shanqiang' or their accurate English equivalents) to describe the structural components?
Does the Wudian diagram clearly depict a hip roof structure with four sloping sides meeting at a central ridge, with no visible vertical gable walls?
Does the Xieshan diagram accurately depict a hip-and-gable structure, showing a hip roof section at the top and triangular gable walls visible underneath the eaves on the sides?
Does the Xuanshan diagram visually show the roof eaves or purlins extending noticeably beyond the plane of the gable wall (overhang)?
Does the Yingshan diagram visually show the roof edge terminating flush with the gable wall, with no structural overhang?
Are specific structural characteristics (such as ridges, slopes, eaves, or gable walls) textually labeled within the generated diagrams as requested?
Are all text labels and annotations within the images legible, correctly spelled, and free from garbled or nonsensical characters?
Are the generated diagrams free from obvious geometric distortions (e.g., disconnected ridges, impossible perspectives) that would hinder structural understanding?
Do the structural features shown in each diagram (e.g., number of ridges, presence of gable walls) strictly match the definitions provided in the accompanying text?
Do all four diagrams maintain a consistent visual style (e.g., line drawing, schematic view) and scale to facilitate direct comparison of the roof types?
Is each diagram placed immediately adjacent to or following the text paragraph that describes that specific roof type?
